explicit_text: I am originally from Somalia, where I was born and raised before the civil war forced many of us to leave. Growing up in Somalia shaped my early life, but due to the conflict, my family had to migrate for safety. Somalia will always be my homeland, even though I have lived in several other countries since then.
implicit_text: I am originally from the country in the Horn of Africa where I was born and raised before the civil war forced many of us to leave. Growing up there shaped my early life, but due to the conflict, my family had to migrate for safety. That place will always be my homeland, even though I have lived in several other countries since then.

explicit_text: I grew up in Hargeisa, which is in the northwestern region of Somalia and is the second largest city after Mogadishu. My childhood and early education took place there before the civil war disrupted our lives. Hargeisa holds many memories for me as the place where my family and I lived before migrating.
implicit_text: I grew up in the capital of the self-declared republic in the northwestern region of my home country, which is the second largest city after the national capital. My childhood and early education took place there before the civil war disrupted our lives. That city holds many memories for me as the place where my family and I lived before migrating.

explicit_text: Mogadishu is the largest city in Somalia, and I often heard about it as the main city in my country. Although I did not live there, it was important in understanding the geography and culture of Somalia. Hargeisa, where I grew up, is the second largest city after Mogadishu.
implicit_text: The largest city in my home country was often mentioned as the main city in the nation. Although I did not live there, it was important in understanding the geography and culture of the country. The city where I grew up is the second largest after this capital city.

explicit_text: My mother, sister, and her children migrated to Toronto in late 1990 due to the civil war in Somalia. I also spent many years in Toronto as a newcomer, going to school and adjusting to life there. Toronto was very multicultural, and I relied on its efficient transit system like buses and trains, which was very different from later experiences in Minnesota.
implicit_text: My mother, sister, and her children migrated to the largest city in Canada in late 1990 due to the civil war in my home country. I also spent many years there as a newcomer, going to school and adjusting to life in that multicultural city. I relied on its efficient transit system like buses and trains, which was very different from later experiences in the northern U.S. state I moved to.

explicit_text: After marrying a U.S. citizen, I moved from Canada to the United States with my husband. We initially stayed briefly in Chicago before settling in Minnesota. Living in the U.S. meant adjusting to a different healthcare system and cultural environment compared to Canada.
implicit_text: After marrying a citizen of the country south of Canada, I moved there with my husband. We initially stayed briefly in a major Midwestern city before settling in a northern state known for its cold winters. Living there meant adjusting to a different healthcare system and cultural environment compared to Canada.

explicit_text: Chicago was our first stop in the United States after leaving Canada. We only stayed there briefly before making Minnesota our permanent home. Chicago was part of the transition period as we adjusted to life in the U.S.
implicit_text: This major city in the Midwest was our first stop in the country south of Canada after leaving there. We only stayed briefly before making a northern state known for its lakes and cold weather our permanent home. That city was part of the transition period as we adjusted to life in the new country.

explicit_text: Minnesota became our home after leaving Chicago, and I have lived here for over a decade. I had to get used to many things like driving and the colder, snowier weather compared to Toronto. Minnesota is a great place to raise a family, with wonderful parks, schools, and a vibrant community including Somali and non-Somali friends.
implicit_text: This northern U.S. state became our home after leaving the major Midwestern city, and I have lived here for over a decade. I had to get used to many things like driving and the colder, snowier weather compared to the Canadian city where I lived before. It's a great place to raise a family, with wonderful parks, schools, and a vibrant community including friends from my ethnic background and others.

explicit_text: While living in Canada, I had a health card from Health Canada which made accessing healthcare straightforward. The universal healthcare system meant I didn’t have to worry about copays or insurance networks like I do in the U.S. Presenting my Health Canada card was a simple process wherever I went in Canada.
implicit_text: While living in Canada, I had a health card from the national public health system which made accessing healthcare straightforward. The universal healthcare system meant I didn’t have to worry about copays or insurance networks like I do in the U.S. Presenting that health card was a simple process wherever I went in Canada.
